evals.name: capture-intent-and-interview
evals.prompt: I want to create a skill that helps generate database migration scripts safely. It should check for destructive operations and suggest rollback plans.
evals.expected_output: Agent asks clarifying questions to narrow the scope before writing anything — what DB engine, what ORM, what safety checks matter most, example scenarios

assertions.text: Asks clarifying questions before writing SKILL.md
Does NOT immediately write a SKILL.md

evals.name: writes-well-structured-skill
evals.prompt: Based on our discussion: the skill is for PostgreSQL migrations using Prisma. It should check for destructive column drops, suggest rollback scripts, and warn about data-dependent migrations. Write the SKILL.md.
evals.expected_output: A SKILL.md with proper frontmatter (name, description, version), trigger-focused description, imperative instructions, under 500 lines

evals.name: creates-test-cases
evals.prompt: Let's test the migration safety skill. Create test cases — realistic prompts that should and shouldn't trigger it.
evals.expected_output: An evals JSON file with 10+ queries mixing should-trigger and should-not-trigger, realistic and detailed per skill-creator guidelines

assertions.text: Mix of should_trigger true and false
Negative cases are near-misses not obviously irrelevant

evals.name: runs-eval-and-reports
evals.prompt: Run the trigger evals on the migration-safety skill and show me the results.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs run_eval.py with proper arguments, reports pass/fail rates, identifies which queries failed

evals.name: iterates-based-on-feedback
evals.prompt: The eval results show the skill doesn't trigger for 'add a new column to the users table' but does trigger for 'write a SQL query'. Fix the description.
evals.expected_output: Agent analyzes the failures, identifies description gaps, rewrites the description to catch migration-related queries without false-triggering on general SQL
